outcome_text: Approaches for mapping AI technology and legal risks of its components—including the use of third-party data or software—are in place, followed, and documented, as are risks of infringement of a third-party's intellectual property or other rights.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI component risk mapping methodology, including assessing legal and technology risks of AI system components, evaluating third-party data and software risks, identifying intellectual property infringement risks, and documenting component-level risk assessments.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI component risk mapping methodology, including assessing legal and technology risks of AI system components, evaluating third-party data and software risks, identifying intellectual property infringement risks, and documenting component-level risk assessments.
reference_document_element_description: Conduct AI system risk classification under the EU AI Act, documenting risk category determination, applicable requirements identification, and conformity assessment pathway selection.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-4.1 (MAP) equal WIDAI RC domain. RC-T-012 is the strongest match (STS: 0.653, cosine: 0.713). Secondary: OP-K-002 (OP, cosine: 0.694), AG-K-010 (AG, cosine: 0.681).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.5/10 (raw 0.653). Shared vocabulary: [ai, documenting, risk, system]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [assessing, assessments, component, components, data, evaluating] vs WIDAI terms [act, applicable, assessment, category, classification, conduct] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.891 (P=0.886, R=0.896). Balanced P/R — the 33-word competency implication and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (89.0%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.713 > STS 0.653 (gap +0.060). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, documenting, risk, system] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.9%. Shared tokens: [ai, documenting, risk, system]. NIST AI RMF-only: [assessing, assessments, component, components, data, evaluating]. WIDAI-only: [act, applicable, assessment, category, classification, conduct].
